Fix histogram metrics with programmatic MeterFilter instead of YAML property

The YAML percentile-histogram property didn't bind correctly for dotted
meter names. Use a MeterFilter bean to reliably enable histogram buckets
for http.server.requests.

fix: use full page navigation for cross-system auth redirects

When returnTo points to a different system (e.g. /idea-machina),
React Router's navigate() stays within RaamattuNytApp's router which
doesn't have that route, causing a 404. Now uses window.location.href
for cross-system URLs so detectActiveSystem() re-runs on full page load.

fix: show error state on Data Sources load failure instead of blank page

Add error/retry UI so a 503 from a bad server start doesn't silently
blank the page. Also fixes: always rebuild static assets in same commit.

commit.message:
Add GBU-57 MOP bunker busters to cost model + OpenClaw vague quantity parsing

- Added GBU-57 Massive Ordnance Penetrator: 50 × $3.5M = $175M
  (based on "dozens of 2,000-pound bombs" from Al Jazeera B-2 report)
- Total cost now $4.2B+ / ~$692M/day
- OpenClaw now parses vague quantity words:
  dozens=50, hundreds=500, scores=40, several=5, numerous=50, etc.
- Explicit numbers still take priority over vague estimates

commit.message:
fix: use SymbolEqualityComparer for symbol comparisons (#997)

## Summary
- Replace `ImmutableArray<IMethodSymbol>.Contains()` with
`IsInstanceOf()` in 2 analyzers
- `Contains()` uses reference equality; `IsInstanceOf()` uses
`SymbolEqualityComparer.Default`
- Fixes false negatives in
`RaiseEventArgumentsShouldMatchEventSignatureAnalyzer` and
`EventSetupHandlerShouldMatchEventTypeAnalyzer`

Closes #973

## Changes
- `RaiseEventArgumentsShouldMatchEventSignatureAnalyzer.cs`: Replace
`knownSymbols.Mock1Raise.Contains(methodSymbol.OriginalDefinition)` with
`methodSymbol.IsInstanceOf(knownSymbols.Mock1Raise)`
- `EventSetupHandlerShouldMatchEventTypeAnalyzer.cs`: Replace
`knownSymbols.ItIsAny.Contains(methodSymbol)` with
`methodSymbol.IsInstanceOf(knownSymbols.ItIsAny)`

## Test plan
- [x] Full test suite passes (2801 tests)
- [x] Build succeeds with 0 warnings

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->

## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Chores**
* Improved the detection mechanism in event handler analyzers to more
accurately identify specific method invocations using refined
symbol-checking logic.

<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

Co-authored-by: Richard Murillo <rjmurillo@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fase 8.D fix: scroll de tiles + FPS limiter

Bug 1 - Arboles/objetos se movian con el jugador:
- Agregar SetViewCrop() a IRenderBackend para replicar el recorte BltFast
- DrawBackground ahora llama SetViewCrop(sModX+offsetX, sModY+offsetY)
- BlitRenderTextureToDDraw lee desde (cropX, cropY) en vez de (0,0)
- Canvas SFML agrandado a res+96 para acomodar tiles extra del PDBGS
- Rama legacy tambien protegida con if(!g_pRenderBackend)

Bug 2 - Desconexiones del servidor por FPS excesivo:
- Agregar frame rate cap ~60 FPS (16ms) en EventLoop
- Sin SFML el DDraw era el cuello de botella; con SFML corria a 1600+ FPS
- El servidor detectaba speed-hack por exceso de paquetes de movimiento
- Sleep(1) cede CPU mientras espera el siguiente frame

commit.message:
perf: replace recursive iterator with Roslyn Descendants() API (#1003)

## Summary
- Replace hand-rolled recursive `GetAllChildOperations` with Roslyn's
built-in `Descendants()` API
- Uses `Descendants()` (not `DescendantsAndSelf()`) to preserve existing
behavior of excluding the root operation
- Removes 16 lines of custom traversal code

Closes #984

## Changes
- `MockRepositoryVerifyAnalyzer.cs`: Replace 2 call sites, delete
`GetAllChildOperations` method
- Net: 2 insertions, 18 deletions

## Review iterations
- Reviewed for correctness (Descendants vs DescendantsAndSelf), import
presence, dead code removal
- PASS: No issues found

## Test plan
- [x] 2801 tests pass (14 MockRepositoryVerify-specific tests)
- [x] Build succeeds with 0 warnings

Fix AttributeError crash: rename self.sim_ctrl to self.simulation_ctrl

Replace 7 remaining references to the old `self.sim_ctrl` attribute name
with `self.simulation_ctrl` in main_window_simulation.py. The property was
renamed in MainWindow but this mixin was not updated, causing an
AttributeError when displaying transient, Monte Carlo, AC sweep, or
DC operating point results.

Fixes #716

commit.message:
fix(skill-publishing): sync plugin CHANGELOGs from source skill

Previously, sync-monorepo.sh preserved plugin CHANGELOGs during resync,
causing them to drift from the source skill's CHANGELOG. Now:
- Auto-resync detects CHANGELOG drift as a trigger condition
- Syncs CHANGELOG.md from source to both plugin skill dir and plugin root
- --add-plugin path no longer preserves stale CHANGELOGs (only README)

commit.message:
Fix OpenClaw false positive: B-2 sorties inflated from 8 to 2,000

OpenClaw misread "2,000-pound bombs" as 2,000 B-2 sorties, inflating
total cost from $4.0B to $14.9B. Fixes:
- Reset B-2 sortie quantity to 8 (correct value)
- Recalculate total cost back to $4.0B+ / ~$663M/day
- Add false-positive suffix filter to extract_number_near_keyword()
  (filters out pound, lb, kg, percent, billion, million, etc.)
- Tighten B-2 keywords to require "sortie" context

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add dynamic language rules injection (lang-rules.md)

Step 0 detects languages/frameworks, then dynamically injects
relevant rules from lang-rules.md into agent prompts.
Covers SQL, .NET, Java, Python, Go, Spring, EF Core.
No new agents needed - existing agents get smarter per-project.

Add OpenAI-compatible provider, preflight checks, and CLI/docs improvements

  OpenAI-compatible endpoint. Keyless local servers supported via
  api_key_env: "none". Handles content: null responses gracefully.
- Pipeline preflight_check() validates RAG and model health before
  deliberation starts, with warnings surfaced in --progress output.
- CLI help text for all 13 subcommands and ~50 arguments, plus --version.
- README rewritten: 8-stage architecture diagram, panel voting docs,
  OpenAI config examples, environment variables table.
- 12 new tests (7 OpenAI client + 3 preflight + 2 edge cases).

Fix all broken sections: remove map, fix Theater/Developments/Evidence/Claims/Polls crash

Root cause: renderTheaterOps() called .join() on a string (not array),
causing a TypeError that crashed the entire loadLiveData() callback,
killing ALL renderers after it (Developments, Evidence, Claims, Polls,
Reactions, Diplomatic, Projections, Breakdown).

Changes:
- Remove map entirely (HTML, CSS, Leaflet imports, all JS map functions)
- Fix theaterOps locations to handle both string and array formats
- Fix groundTroops poll missing label fallback
- Add chart resize on cost tab switch (fixes hidden canvas rendering)
- Update hardcoded cost fallback values to match current data

Add PT dosing, cardiac reconditioning, SFN, cartilage scaffolds, advocacy, dental implants, and LDN sections

New content across 6 pages based on research findings R325-R331:
- treatment/exercise.html: PT dosing framework (2-3x/week, 30-45min, 10% rule, closed-chain preferred, red flags) and cardiac reconditioning protocol (3-phase recumbent-to-upright GEAR framework)
- living/autonomic-dysfunction.html: Small fiber neuropathy section (61-100% prevalence in hEDS, skin biopsy gold standard, IVIG for autoimmune SFN)
- future/tissue-engineering.html: Cartilage scaffold section (collagen XI chondrogenic properties, bilayer scaffolds, iPSC-derived gene-corrected chondrocytes)
- resources/index.html: Advocacy organizations (NORD, EveryLife, Global Genes, SIP, Give Kids a Chance Act 2025, RDCRN)
- living/dental-orthodontic.html: Dental implant section (osseointegration challenges, extended healing, bone quality assessment, surface modifications)
- treatment/pain-management.html: LDN section (TLR4 mechanism, 66.7% benefit in HSD/EDS, prescribing protocol)

fix: remove corrupted for-loop fragment in init_db crashing startup

The attention_sync_meta table insertion left a dangling `for ddl in [`
with no list items and no closing bracket, causing a runtime error on
every server start — init_db() would raise NameError, leaving the
user_types table unpopulated and all /api/* endpoints returning errors.

Removed the dead fragment; the HNSW index try/except below already
handles the only statement that loop was meant to wrap.

Remove all time and duration mentions from content

commit.message:
Overhaul filter pipeline: target scheduled binary events, not nominees

Complete rewrite of the filtering logic per new spec:

Hard filters now exclude:
- Sports: expanded to 50+ keywords covering leagues, esports, match
  patterns ("vs"), player props, category-level filtering
- Awards: Oscar/Grammy/Emmy/SAG/BAFTA/etc + "best X" patterns
- Elections: primary/nomination/runoff/seat patterns
- Multi-outcome: event_group_size > 2 (standalone or YES/NO pair only)

LLM prompt rewritten to target monotonic-potential binary events:
- Threshold/milestone markets (Bitcoin hits X, GDP exceeds Y)
- Policy/regulatory (Fed cuts rates, bill passes Senate)
- Occurrence (hurricane, layoffs, strikes)
- Deadline-based (will X happen before Y date)

Back to 100K markets. Hard filter: 100K -> ~276 clean candidates for LLM.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

10 expert agents, 5 execution modes, 3-tier model routing,
AgentSpeak protocol, self-correction loop, persistent learning.

commit.message:
Fix: close() 종료 전 CPU resume 보장

halt 상태로 J-Link를 닫으면 SSD 펌웨어가 영구 정지.
Ctrl+C 등 종료 시 _go_with_retry()로 CPU를 반드시 resume 후 jlink.close().

fix: strip /api suffix from ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL before constructing API URL
